description: Curated per-text demo subset for the M6 model-diffing experiment. Each example carries the precomputed BOS-excluded max-pooled co-response-UNIT and best-single-latent responses on gemma-2-2b (base) vs gemma-2-2b-it (it) at layer-12 through the shared frozen Gemma-Scope pt-SAE. The demo re-runs the downstream diffing statistics (AUC, sign-flip null, doc-bootstrap CIs, B2 control-floor genuine shift) on these arrays.

member_labels.1920: references to specific biological terms and acronyms
member_labels.14630: character names and terms in various languages, particularly those with diacritics

sanity.note: AUC>0.5 => unit fires more on toxic than neutral within each model (concept present).

reference_results.headline_finding: M6 bounded model-diffing result. The shared BASE-trained Gemma-Scope pt-SAE reconstructs gemma-2-2b-it layer-12 activations almost as well as gemma-2-2b (IT recon cosine=0.913 vs base 0.925; not catastrophic), so the shared-SAE diffing recipe is viable here. A base-vs-IT activation difference IS detectable above the model-label shuffle null for the toxicity unit (AUC=0.438, departure 0.062, direction=it>base). BUT it is NOT concept-specific: the first-letter-spelling CONTROL shows the same direction and a comparable departure (0.062, dir=it>base), and the IT residual-stream norm is 1.11x the base norm. After subtracting the control floor the GENUINE toxicity shift is +0.000 (95% CI [-0.009,0.007]); norm-matched genuine +0.027 (CI [0.021,0.033]). The co-response UNIT does NOT detect the shift more reliably than the best single latent (anchor 12714); abs-dev diff CI [-0.0007237152777777644, 0.007657699652777816]. Note the direction (IT fires the toxicity concept MORE, not less) is opposite the naive detox prediction and is explained by generic OOD/norm drift, not a concept-specific reduction in toxicity-feature usage.
